title: Blood lead changes during pregnancy and postpartum with calcium supplementation.
ehr_evidence: The study used monthly venous blood samples, quarterly environmental samples, and 6-day duplicate diets rather than electronic health records.
summary: A longitudinal study investigating the effect of calcium supplementation on lead mobilization from the maternal skeleton during pregnancy and 6 months postpartum in immigrant women in Australia.
key_findings: Blood lead levels increased by a geometric mean of 25% during the third trimester.
Calcium supplementation was provided to evaluate its protective effect against lead mobilization.
population: 10 immigrant women in Australia (pregnant/lactating) and 2 controls
